payload.description: Webster Avenue corridor based on the existing Bx41 route, 5.3 miles from The Hub to Williamsbridge
Bx41 LTD converted to Bx41 SBS with 1/2 mile stop spacing, frequent all-day service
Bx41 Local service with no change to stop spacing, service every ~10 minutes

tool_args.label: Offset bus lanes on Webster Avenue

payload.description: 4 miles of offset bus lanes with bus bulb stations on Webster Avenue
Off board fare collection for the entire Bx41 SBS route
Transit signal priority for the entire Bx41 SBS route
SBS stations with bus bulbs for high-quality stations
Pedestrian safety improvements including curb extensions, medians, pedestrian refuge islands, and neckdowns
Low-floor buses for the entire Bx41 SBS route
Station and bus branding for the entire Bx41 SBS route

payload.claim_text: Within a 10-minute walk of the Webster Avenue corridor: 200,000 residents, 71% of households do not own a car, 61% of residents commute by transit
payload.subject: Webster Avenue corridor

payload.claim_text: Community top priorities: 1. Bx41 bus service, 2. Pedestrian safety, 3. Curb access and parking, 4. Accommodating future development
payload.subject: Webster Avenue SBS project community input

payload.claim_text: Visited over 150 businesses along the corridor and interviewed 22 nearby institutions
payload.subject: Webster Avenue SBS business outreach

payload.claim_text: More than 50% of businesses identified space for deliveries and customer parking as very important curb access needs
payload.subject: Webster Avenue corridor businesses

payload.claim_text: Almost 70% of businesses get deliveries daily. The most common delivery time is between 7am and noon.
payload.subject: Webster Avenue corridor businesses

payload.claim_text: 79% of businesses have deliveries that take 30 minutes or less. 72% of businesses estimate that customers park for less than 30 minutes.
payload.subject: Webster Avenue corridor businesses

warnings: metric_claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids on metric payloads refer to route entities and should become explicit relations when supported. Suggested relation: route has_metric metric_claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.
